description: Curated seed of core financial/market-moving entities for KG bootstrap. Each entry maps 1:1 to a KnowledgeStore entity; entity_type must match the EntityType enum in knowledge_graph.models.

entities.description: Central bank of the United States. Sets the federal funds rate, supervises banks, and is the dominant driver of global dollar liquidity.
Central bank of the euro area, based in Frankfurt. Sets monetary policy for the 20 euro-using EU member states.
Central bank of the United Kingdom. Sets UK monetary policy via the Monetary Policy Committee and supervises UK financial stability.
Central bank of Japan. Known for decades of ultra-loose monetary policy and yield curve control; a major driver of JPY and global carry trades.
Central bank of the People's Republic of China. Manages the yuan (CNY) and Chinese monetary policy under state direction.
Central bank of Switzerland. Manages the Swiss franc and is known for large FX interventions.
Central bank of Australia. Sets the cash rate target and manages the Australian dollar.
Central bank of Canada. Sets the overnight rate and manages the Canadian dollar.
Central bank of India. Sets the repo rate and manages the Indian rupee.
US federal agency regulating securities markets, public companies, and investment advisors.
US federal agency regulating derivatives markets, including futures and swaps.
UK regulator overseeing financial services firms and markets conduct.
EU authority coordinating securities market regulation across member states.
ECB-led banking supervision arm for the euro area. Directly supervises large and cross-border euro-area banks; the regulator face of the ECB distinct from its monetary policy role.
Ministerial-level regulator of China's securities and futures markets; approves IPOs and sets market rules for A-shares.
German federal regulator for banking, insurance, and securities markets; Germany's primary financial markets supervisor.
Singapore's integrated financial regulator and de facto central bank. Supervises banks, capital markets, insurers, and payments; manages the SGD exchange-rate policy band.
Washington-based multilateral lender that provides balance-of-payments support and publishes influential global economic forecasts.
Multilateral development bank providing loans and grants to low- and middle-income countries.
Basel-based institution serving as bank for central banks and host of the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision.

entities.canonical_name: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
entities.description: Paris-based intergovernmental organisation of mostly high-income economies; publishes economic outlooks and policy benchmarks.
Largest stock exchange in the world by market capitalisation, based in New York. Owned by Intercontinental Exchange.
US electronic stock exchange dominated by large technology listings.
Primary stock exchange of the United Kingdom, based in London. Home to FTSE index listings.
Primary stock exchange of Japan, operated by Japan Exchange Group.
Primary stock exchange of Hong Kong and a major gateway for trading in Chinese equities.
US multinational consumer electronics and services company headquartered in Cupertino, California. One of the largest companies by market capitalisation.
US multinational technology company based in Redmond, Washington. Major cloud, software, and AI platform provider.
Parent company of Google, headquartered in Mountain View, California. Dominant in search, online advertising, and cloud infrastructure.
US multinational technology and e-commerce company headquartered in Seattle. Owner of AWS, the largest public cloud provider.
US semiconductor company dominant in GPUs used for AI, gaming, and data centres.
US technology company operating Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Reality Labs. Headquartered in Menlo Park, California.
US electric vehicle and clean energy company led by Elon Musk, based in Austin, Texas.
US multinational conglomerate holding company led by Warren Buffett; large insurance, railroad, energy, and equity portfolio.
Largest US bank by assets, offering investment banking, consumer banking, and asset management.
Major US diversified financial services company headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina.
US multinational investment bank and financial services firm based in New York.
US multinational oil and gas corporation, one of the largest publicly traded energy companies.
Saudi state-owned petroleum and natural gas company; one of the largest companies in the world by revenue and market capitalisation.
Taiwan-based contract chip manufacturer; world's largest dedicated semiconductor foundry.
World's largest asset manager by AUM, headquartered in New York. Operator of the iShares ETF family.
US investment adviser and index-fund pioneer based in Valley Forge, Pennsylvania.
US credit rating agency; one of the 'Big Three' along with S&P and Fitch.
Credit rating agency subsidiary of S&P Global; one of the 'Big Three'.
Credit rating agency; one of the 'Big Three' major rating agencies.
Chair of the US Federal Reserve since 2018. Previously a Fed governor and partner at The Carlyle Group.
President of the European Central Bank since 2019. Former Managing Director of the IMF and French finance minister.
Governor of the Bank of England since 2020.
Governor of the Bank of Japan since April 2023. Academic economist appointed to lead normalisation of BOJ policy.
US Treasury Secretary 2021-2025. Former Chair of the Federal Reserve.
Chair and CEO of Berkshire Hathaway; widely followed value investor known as the 'Oracle of Omaha'.
CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, owner of X (formerly Twitter). Major market-moving figure in tech and EV sectors.
Chair and CEO of JPMorgan Chase since 2005. Influential voice on US banking and macroeconomic policy.
US large-cap equity index of 500 leading companies; the dominant benchmark for US equities.
Price-weighted index of 30 large US public companies.
Market-cap weighted index of all stocks listed on the Nasdaq exchange; heavily skewed to technology.
Index of the 100 largest non-financial Nasdaq-listed companies. Tracked by the QQQ ETF.
US small-cap equity index of 2,000 smallest companies in the Russell 3000.
Index of the 100 largest companies listed on the London Stock Exchange.
Blue-chip stock market index of the 40 largest German companies on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange.
Benchmark French stock market index of the 40 largest Euronext Paris listings.
Price-weighted index of 225 large Japanese companies on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.
Benchmark Hong Kong equity index of the largest companies listed on HKEX.
Index of all A-shares and B-shares listed on the Shanghai Stock Exchange.
Broad European equity index covering 600 large, mid, and small caps across 17 countries.
Measure of expected 30-day volatility of the S&P 500 implied from options pricing; the 'fear gauge'.
Benchmark Indian equity index of 50 large-cap NSE-listed companies; the headline gauge for Indian equities alongside the BSE Sensex.
Korea Composite Stock Price Index; market-cap weighted index of all common stocks on the Korea Exchange, dominated by Samsung, SK, and Hyundai groups.
Benchmark Canadian equity index of the largest Toronto Stock Exchange listings; heavy concentration in financials and energy.
Benchmark Australian equity index of the 200 largest ASX-listed companies; dominated by mining and banking names.
Benchmark Italian equity index of the 40 largest Borsa Italiana listings; the primary gauge for Italian equities.
Benchmark Brazilian equity index; the headline gauge for B3 (São Paulo) listings and the largest emerging-market Latin American index.
Official currency of the United States and the world's primary reserve currency.
Official currency of the euro area; second most-held reserve currency after the USD.
Official currency of the United Kingdom.
Official currency of Japan; major funding currency for global carry trades.
Official currency of the People's Republic of China; also called the renminbi.
Official currency of Switzerland, widely held as a safe-haven asset.
Official currency of Australia; a risk-on / commodity-linked major often moved by iron ore and China demand.
Official currency of Canada; a commodity currency closely tracking crude oil prices.
Official currency of Hong Kong, pegged to the US dollar via the Linked Exchange Rate System since 1983.
Official currency of Singapore, managed by the Monetary Authority of Singapore against an undisclosed basket.
Official currency of Sweden; a high-beta European major frequently paired against the euro.
Official currency of South Korea; emerging-market major heavily tied to export and semiconductor flows.
Official currency of Norway; a commodity currency sensitive to crude oil and Norges Bank policy.
Official currency of New Zealand; commodity-linked major, often moved alongside AUD.
Official currency of India; managed-float emerging-market currency under Reserve Bank of India oversight.
First and largest cryptocurrency by market capitalisation; decentralised digital asset often framed as 'digital gold'.
Second-largest cryptocurrency by market capitalisation; programmable smart-contract platform.
West Texas Intermediate crude oil; benchmark grade for North American oil pricing.
North Sea crude oil blend; primary benchmark for global oil pricing.
Precious metal widely held as a monetary reserve and inflation hedge.
Precious metal with both monetary and industrial demand.
Industrial metal whose price is often treated as a barometer of global economic activity ('Dr. Copper').
Hydrocarbon gas used for heating, electricity generation, and industry; benchmark prices include Henry Hub and TTF.
US government debt instrument maturing in 10 years; its yield is the primary benchmark for global risk-free rates.
Long-dated German government bond, typically the 10-year benchmark for euro-area risk-free rates.
Measure of the average change over time in prices paid by consumers for a basket of goods and services. Main headline inflation gauge.
Consumer Price Index excluding food and energy; preferred gauge for underlying inflation trends.
US inflation measure preferred by the Federal Reserve for its 2% target, published by the BEA.
Measure of the average change in selling prices received by domestic producers; early indicator of consumer inflation pressures.
Monthly US jobs report released by the BLS; headline number excludes farm, government, and non-profit employees.
Share of the labour force that is unemployed and actively seeking work.
Weekly US data on new filings for unemployment insurance; high-frequency labour market indicator.
Total monetary value of goods and services produced within a country in a given period; headline measure of economic output.
Monthly estimate of consumer spending at retail establishments; key real-time indicator of consumption.
Monthly US survey of purchasing managers in manufacturing; readings above 50 indicate expansion.
Interest rate at which US depository institutions lend reserves to each other overnight; the Fed's primary policy rate.
Rate at which commercial banks are remunerated for overnight deposits at the ECB; the euro area's main policy rate since 2014.
Difference between the monetary value of a country's exports and imports over a period.
